variants.pronounced: さっぽろしりつだいがく
さっぽろかんくきしょうだい
さっぽろがくいんだいがく
さっぽろこくさいだいがく
さっぽろいかだいがく
さっぽろおおたにだいがく

glosses: card (oft. featuring illustrations of sweets) used to represent money when gambling
card that can be counted as more than one type of card for scoring combinations (e.g. the sake cup card or the November suit)
card that can be used to make a scoring combination
card to be read (e.g. in hyakunin isshu karuta, iroha karuta)
card to pick up (esp. in hyakunin isshu karuta, i.e. the card printed with the second half of a poem)
card used to pick up the remaining three cards of the same suit in the field

variants.pronounced: きょうそうにゅうさつ
さいこうがくにゅうさつしゃ

glosses: holding two positions at once (esp. of rikishi or judges who are also coaches)
inspection of tickets in the car (carriage)
paper money issued by a feudal domain
sign on a post (esp. containing information about a sight, warnings, congratulations, etc.)
sign staked to a building's ridgepole at construction time stating the building's donor, builder, date, reason for construction, etc.
slips of paper posted on shrine pillars by pilgrims
ticket examiner (collector, inspector) (in a railroad station)
to give a signed statement or an IOU
to make a bid (e.g. at an auction)

variants.pronounced: にゅうさつをあたえる

glosses: type of amulet sold at Shinto shrines
type of household amulet or talisman, issued by a Shinto shrine, hung in the house for protection
votive tablet donated to a shrine or temple
wooden signboard with a peaked top (usu. with information about a historical place)
collusion before a bid or tender

variants.pronounced: にゅうさつせっけいずしょ

glosses: kurifuda (type of playing cards numbered 1-6; used by the bank in tehonbiki)

variants.pronounced: さいていらくさつかかく

glosses: stripped hanafuda deck consisting of 40 cards

variants.pronounced: いっぱんきょうそうにゅうさつ

glosses: cards placed face up on the table
playing cards consisting of 12 suits of 4 cards, each suit representing a month indicated by a flower

variants.pronounced: あいさつよりえんさつ

glosses: tag displaying a base price which cannot be reduced by negotiation

variants.pronounced: にゅうさついらいしょ

glosses: sign on a post (usu. wooden and with a small roof, containing information about a sight, warning, congratulations, etc.)
batons (playing card suit in traditional Japanese gambling decks)
bulletin board displaying the name, age, offence, etc. of a criminal sentenced to death (Edo period)
mekuri karuta (card game popular in the late Edo period)
harifuda (type of playing cards numbered 1-6; used for playing tehonbiki)
iron tablet belonging to Yama that is inscribed with the names of souls to be sent to hell
kabufuda (type of playing cards used to play oicho-kabu)
tag verifying merchant guild membership (Edo period)
picture card (in utagaruta or iroha karuta)
putting up a sign forbidding entry, passage, etc.
swords (playing card suit in traditional Japanese gambling decks)
red tag (esp. one attached to sold goods or discounted products)
tag verifying one has permission to take plants and trees from common land (Edo period)
deck (of playing cards on table, face down, from which cards are drawn)
votive tablet donated to a shrine or temple
extra card (in hanafuda, kabufuda, etc.; sometimes depicting an ogre)
announcement flier (e.g. sale at a shop)
hikifuda (type of playing cards numbered 1-6, used by the bank in tehonbiki)
honour awarded to samurai in Tosa
golden tablet belonging to Yama that is inscribed with the names of souls to be sent to paradise
slip of paper posted on shrine pillars by pilgrims
